item.title: Madison Air raises $2.2 billion in US IPO
item.description: Indoor air systems provider Madison Air Solutions said on Wednesday it had raised $2.23 billion in its U.S. initial public вҖӢoffering.

item.title: Trading Day: S&P 500, Nasdaq nab all-time closing highs, buoyed by Middle East optimism
item.description: The S&P 500 and the Nasdaq both logged record closing highs on Wednesday as investors assessed developments in the U.S.-Iran conflict and turned their focus to corporate earnings.

item.title: Nasdaq sets record highs as investors return to tech stocks
item.description: The Nasdaq hit an intraday all-time high and closing record on Wednesday, its first since October, as investors returned to technology stocks, signaling a rebound вҖӢin risk appetite after weeks of concerns over war-related economic disruption and вҖҢfears about the labor-force impact of AI.

item.title: Trump issues several pipeline permits for US-Canada oil transportation
item.description: U.S. President вҖӢDonald Trump issued several pipeline permits вҖҢon Wednesday, including one for the construction of a new pipeline, to facilitate the transportation of вҖӢcrude oil and petroleum products between вҖӢthe U.S. and Canada, according to documents вҒ released by the White House.

item.title: Nuclear reactor developer XвҖ‘Energy targets $7.5 billion valuation in US IPO
item.description: Amazon-backed X-Energy said on Wednesday it was targeting a valuation of up to $7.51 billion in вҖӢits initial public offering in the United States.

item.title: SEC approves rule change to allow customer cross-margining in Treasury market
item.description: The вҖӢU.S. вҖҢSecurities and Exchange вҖӢCommission вҖӢon Wednesday вҒ said вҖӢit approved вҖӢan exemptive order вҖӢand вҖӢproposed rule change вҖҢto вҒ permit customer cross-margining вҖӢin вҖӢthe вҒ U.S. Treasury вҖӢmarket.

item.title: VIEW S&P 500, Nasdaq notch fresh records, casting aside war fears
item.description: U.S. stock indexes returned to new highs on Wednesday, putting fears of economic disruption driven by the war with Iran in the rearview mirror.

item.title: Ford's advanced EV-and-tech chief to leave the company
item.description: Doug Field, a Tesla and вҖӢApple veteran who has led Ford Motor's electric-vehicle and technology efforts for nearly five years, is leaving вҖҢthe automaker.

item.title: IMF says Bank of Japan can see through inflationary shock from Iran war
item.description: The Bank of Japan can see through inflationary pressures from the war in the Middle East as any second-round effects on broader prices will be limited, Rahul Anand, the International Monetary Fund's mission chief for Japan, told Reuters on Wednesday.

item.title: Bessent supports IMF quota revamp, wants World Bank financing for critical minerals
item.description: U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent on Wednesday reaffirmed his support for a long-delayed revamp of the International Monetary Fund's quota resources and called on the World Bank to move quickly to support projects to develop critical вҖӢminerals to diversify supplies away from China.

item.title: Permian-focused EagleRock plans rare US oil and gas IPO, sources say
item.description: EagleRock, a company that collects royalties and fees from oil and gas вҖҢproduction on the land it controls in the Permian Basin, is exploring a U.S. initial public offering that could value it at up to $2 billion, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: NY jury finds Live Nation illegally monopolized ticketing markets, Bloomberg News reports
item.description: Live Nation illegally monopolized вҖӢticketing markets, a New вҖҢYork federal jury found Wednesday, Bloomberg News reported.

item.title: Exclusive: Fed's Musalem says oil shock likely to keep core inflation near 3%, rates on hold for some time
item.description: High oil prices are likely to keep underlying inflation nearly a percentage point above the Federal Reserve's вҖӢ2% target for the rest of this year, with the U.S. central bank likely needing to leave interest rates unchanged, St. Louis Fed President Alberto Musalem said on Wednesday.

item.title: Sazerac offers to buy Jack Daniel's maker Brown-Forman for $15 billion, WSJ reports
item.description: U.S. spirits group Sazerac has offered to buy Jack Daniel's вҖӢmaker Brown-Forman for about $15 billion, the вҖҢWall Street Journal reported on Wednesday, citing people familiar with the matter.

item.title: US probes suspicious oil trades made before Trump Iran pivots, source says
item.description: The U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission is investigating вҖӢa series of oil futures trades that were placed вҖҢshortly before major policy shifts by U.S. President Donald Trump related to the war in Iran, a person familiar with the matter said on Wednesday .

item.title: Trump-backed World Liberty Financial's early investors wonвҖҷt be able to fully cash out until after TrumpвҖҷs term
item.description: World Liberty Financial, the crypto venture co-founded вҖӢby President Trump and his sons, released a new proposal Wednesday that would prevent early investors from trading tokens вҖ“ 80% of their holdings вҖҢare currently locked by the firm вҖ“ for two years, followed by an additional two-year vesting period, according to a statement posted on its governance forum.

item.title: Exclusive: European buyers hold talks to ship Canadian LNG via Panama Canal to diversify supply
item.description: European buyers, including Germany's Uniper, are exploring the possibility of purchasing liquefied вҖӢnatural gas from Canada's Pacific coast and shipping it through the Panama Canal as part of a long-term strategy to diversify supply, вҖҢan effort made more urgent in the wake of the Iran war, two sources familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Iran war puts US companies into wait-and-see mode, Fed report says
item.description: U.S. economic activity increased and employment was steady in recent weeks, the вҖӢFederal Reserve said on Wednesday in a report that also pointed to notable impacts from a surge вҖҢin energy prices amid the ongoing Iran war.

item.title: White House has no opinion on potential United Airlines deal for American Airlines
item.description: The Trump administration does not have an opinion on reports вҖӢthat United Airlines was considering merging вҖҢwith rival American Airlines , White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt told reporters on Wednesday.

item.title: Bessent says he is optimistic Warsh will be Fed chair 'on time'
item.description: U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott вҖҢBessent on Wednesday said he was optimistic that Kevin вҖӢWarsh, President Donald вҖӢTrump's nominee for Federal Reserve вҒ Chair, would assume вҖӢthe post on time.

item.title: BoE's Bailey says progress on global stablecoin standards has slowed
item.description: Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey said on Wednesday that progress on establishing вҖӢinternational standards for so-called stablecoins, cryptoassets which вҖҢare pegged to a fiat currency such as the dollar, had slowed over the last year.

item.title: S&P 500 hits first intraday record high since US-Iran war
item.description: The S&P 500 touched an intraday record high on Wednesday, its first since the U.S.-Iran conflict began, as hopes of a de-escalation in the war вҖӢand robust earnings expectations drew investors back into risk assets.

item.title: Morgan Stanley eyes capital relief as lobbying push pays off
item.description: Morgan Stanley's capital requirements will remain вҖӢflat or fall modestly under an overhaul of the U.S. capital regime, its Chief Financial Officer Sharon Yeshaya told Reuters, a вҖҢwin for the Wall Street bank that has pushed hard for reforms.

item.title: Exclusive: Two Sigma, D.E. Shaw join Wall Street push against US SEC's bid to relax quarterly reporting
item.description: Two Sigma Investments and D.E. Shaw are among several Wall Street investment firms and hedge funds that are fighting a proposal to allow companies to opt out of вҖӢquarterly reporting, according to people familiar with the matter, on concerns it would reduce the flow of key financial information for investors in public companies.

item.title: Cadence, Nvidia working together on developing AI for robotics
item.description: Cadence Design Systems вҖӢand Nvidia are partnering to further вҖҢthe development of artificial intelligence for robots, the CEOs of the two companies said on Wednesday.

item.title: Europe sees record jet inflows from US in April
item.description: Europe was seeing record inflows of jet fuel from the United States, data from Kpler and LSEG showed, as the region attempts to shore up fuel supplies to make up for the disruption вҖӢof imports from the Middle East.

item.title: Wall Street still betting on 2026 deal boom, but Middle East unrest adds caution
item.description: Wall Street's biggest banks still expect 2026 to be a strong year for dealmaking, although turmoil in the Middle East вҖӢthat has roiled global markets has tempered executive optimism.

item.title: L3Harris announces $1.27 billion expansion project at Virginia site
item.description: L3Harris Technologies and Virginia Governor Abigail Spanberger said on Wednesday the defense supplier will вҖӢinvest $1.27 billion to further the solid rocket вҖҢmotor production capacity at its Orange County site.

item.title: Tax enforcement weakened after Trump job cuts, IRS data shows
item.description: The Trump administration dramatically reduced efforts to pursue tax cheats in 2025, вҖӢgovernment data shows, shedding tens of thousands of employees at the Internal Revenue Service, including those charged with вҖҢenforcement.

item.title: Robinhood, Webull jump after US SEC approves removal of day-trading limit for smaller investors
item.description: Shares of retail trading platforms Robinhood and Webull surged on Wednesday after the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission paved the way for a new regulatory вҖӢframework on day-trading limit for smaller investors.
